history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing north, and directly ahead is lava (L). To the left is a wall (#), so turning left would not lead to a safe move. To the right, there are empty floor cells (.). I will turn right to face east, then I can move forward onto the safe floor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. Directly ahead is a floor cell ('.'), and the path continues with floor cells for at least two more steps. The goal is not yet visible, but moving forward is safe and explores new area. No need to turn as the surrounding cells are either lava or wall.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. Directly ahead is a safe floor cell ('.'), while left is lava and right is a wall. The goal is not yet visible, but moving forward is safe and allows further exploration of the unseen cells ahead.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly in front (row above) is a safe floor ('.'), while the left cell is lava ('L') and the right cell is a wall ('#'). The goal is not yet visible, so moving forward is the only safe action to continue exploring northward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is a safe floor ('.'), while the immediate left is lava and right is a wall. The goal is not yet visible. Moving forward is the only safe action to advance and explore. Note that after this move, the forward cell will be lava, so a turn will be needed.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is lava ('L'), and to my right (east) is a wall ('#'), so moving forward or turning right and moving forward would be deadly or blocked. The cell to my left (west) is a safe floor ('.'), so I should turn left to face that direction and then move forward to explore.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead (north) is a safe floor '.', while the right cell is lava 'L' and left is floor '.'. Moving forward seems safe and brings us closer to the visible goal at the top. Since the immediate path north appears clear, moving forward is the best step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center, facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is a safe floor '.', while the cells to the left and right are lava 'L'. The goal 'G' is visible further north. Moving forward is safe and progresses toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead (north) is floor '.', while left and right are lava 'L'. The goal 'G' is visible farther north. Moving forward is safe and progresses toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center facing north. The cell directly ahead is floor '.' (safe), while left and right are lava. The goal 'G' is visible to the northeast. Moving forward will step onto a safe floor cell and move closer to the goal. The path ahead appears clear: after this step, the agent can continue north, then turn east to reach the goal while avoiding the lava at (3,4).
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center, facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is floor (`.`), which is safe. The cells to the left and right are lava (`L`). The goal `G` is visible two steps ahead and two steps to the right. Moving forward now will position the agent on a safe floor cell, enabling a future path east then north to reach the goal while avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal G is at row5, col5. My current cell (row6, col3) has safe floor to the north and east, but lava to the west. Moving forward north would place me at (row5, col3), which is a safe floor cell but bounded by lava to left/right and a wall to the north—a dead end. Instead, I can travel east along row6: the cells (6,4) and (6,5) are visible floor. From (6,5), I can turn north and move forward to the goal (5,5) safely. Therefore, I should first turn right to face east.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell directly ahead (5,3) is a safe floor tile. The goal G is visible at (4,2), and a safe path leads north to (5,3), then north to (4,3), then west to the goal, avoiding all lava and walls. Moving forward now is safe and makes progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell directly ahead (5,3) is a safe floor cell (`.`). Moving forward will position the agent closer to the goal `G` at (5,2), allowing a subsequent turn and move to reach it. The cells to the left and right are lava, so turning first is unsafe; moving forward is the only safe progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell directly ahead (5,3) is a wall `#`, so cannot move forward. The goal `G` is immediately to the left at (6,2). Turning left (west) will position the agent to move forward into the goal on the next step. No lava or wall blocks this path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at (6,3) facing north. The cell directly ahead (5,3) is the goal `G`. Moving forward will reach the goal safely with no lava or walls in the way.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
